0_Overview: What is Python: High-level, Interpreted, Dynamically Typed Language
Benefits: Beginner-friendly — Readable, Clean Syntax
Benefits: Largest Ecosystem for Data Science, ML, AI
Benefits: Rapid Prototyping and Development Speed
Benefits: Huge Standard Library and Third-party Packages (PyPI)
Benefits: Cross-platform
Benefits: Versatile: Web, Scripting, Automation, Data, ML, DevOps
Drawbacks: Slow Execution Speed (GIL, interpreted)
Drawbacks: GIL Limits True Multi-threading
Drawbacks: High Memory Usage
Drawbacks: Not Ideal for Mobile Development
Drawbacks: Dynamic Typing Can Hide Bugs (use mypy)
When to Use: Data Science, Machine Learning, AI
When to Use: Scripting and Automation
When to Use: Web Backends (Django, FastAPI, Flask)
When to Use: Rapid Prototyping
When to Use: DevOps and Infrastructure Tooling
When NOT to Use: Mobile Apps, High-performance Systems, Frontend
Python vs JavaScript: Backend Comparison
Python vs R: Data Science Comparison
Python vs Go: Performance vs Ease of Use
Python 2 vs Python 3: Always Use Python 3
CPython vs PyPy vs Jython vs MicroPython
1_Python_Basics: Python Syntax: Indentation, Comments, Variables, Dynamic Typing
Data Types: int, float, complex, str, bool, bytes, bytearray, None
Type Conversion: int(), float(), str(), bool(), list(), tuple(), set(), dict()
String: Indexing, Slicing, Methods (upper, lower, strip, split, join, replace, find)
f-strings: Expressions, Format Specs, = for Debugging
Lists: Indexing, Slicing, append, extend, insert, remove, pop, sort, reverse
List Comprehensions: [expr for x in iterable if condition]
Tuples: Immutability, Packing, Unpacking, Named Tuples
Dictionaries: CRUD, keys(), values(), items(), get(), setdefault(), update()
Sets: add, remove, union, intersection, difference, symmetric_difference
Control Flow: if-elif-else, for, while, break, continue, pass, else on loops
Functions: def, return, default args, *args, **kwargs, keyword-only args
Built-ins: len, range, enumerate, zip, map, filter, sorted, reversed, any, all, sum
Unpacking: *, ** in assignments and function calls
2_Python_OOP: Instance Methods, Class Methods (@classmethod), Static Methods (@staticmethod)
Inheritance: super(), Method Resolution Order (MRO), C3 Linearization
3_Python_Advanced: Decorators: Function Decorators, functools.wraps, Decorator Factories
Generators: yield, yield from, Generator Expressions
itertools: chain, product, combinations, permutations, groupby, islice, cycle
functools: reduce, partial, lru_cache, cache, cached_property, total_ordering
asyncio: Event Loop, Coroutines, Tasks, Futures
async def, await, async for, async with
Threading: Thread, Lock, RLock, Condition, Semaphore, Event
Multiprocessing: Process, Pool, Queue, Pipe, Manager
GIL (Global Interpreter Lock): Impact on Threading
Type Hints: PEP 484, typing module, Python 3.10+ X | Y syntax
Memory Management: Reference Counting, Cyclic GC, gc module
4_Python_Web_Frameworks: FastAPI: Path Operations, Request/Response Models, Pydantic Validation
FastAPI: Background Tasks, Middleware, OAuth2 with JWT
Django: ORM — Models, QuerySets, Managers, Migrations
Django: Admin Interface, URL Routing, Views (FBV, CBV)
Django REST Framework (DRF): Serializers, ViewSets, Routers
Flask: Application Factory, Blueprints, Request/App Context
Celery: Task Queues, Workers, Beat Scheduler, Chains, Groups
Gunicorn and Uvicorn for Production Serving
5_Data_Science_with_Python: Pandas: Filtering, Groupby, Merge, Pivot, Melt
Pandas: Handling Missing Data: dropna, fillna
Model Evaluation: Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1, ROC-AUC
6_Python_Ecosystem: pip, venv, pipenv, Poetry, conda, uv
File I/O: open(), pathlib.Path, os, shutil
pytest: Fixtures, Parametrize, Marks, Conftest, Mocking
SQLAlchemy Core and ORM, Alembic for Migrations
Pydantic v2: BaseModel, Field, Validators, model_validator
